feedback.text: I request that in addition to the current contents of the Digital Fairness Act, the protection of the playable state of videogames with an upfront cost be included. Often times a product will be sold to the consumer at a premium price, and later the product will be rendered unusable due to shutdown servers or other form of end of support to the product. Forcing companies selling these products to leave videogames in a playable state after ending support can only lead to a more fair market for the consumer, and therefore seems like a valid addition to the Digital Fairness Act.
